name: Corporate Charter Office
desc: A colonial charter grants one favored corporation a protected seat in local government, preferred contracts, and the right to operate a dedicated procurement channel. Only one such office can hold enough influence to matter on a given colony, and each corporation brings a different storefront: Zenith services, Yunze weapons, Monolith warships, or Flicker aviation.

name: Zenith Era Charter Office
desc: Zenith Era does not arrive with soldiers or smokestacks. It arrives with auditors, architects, hospitality factors, and immaculate legal staff. A Zenith charter turns the colony into a respectable address for premium services, smoothing disputes before they become scandals and teaching local administration how to make prosperity look inevitable. Its Crown Trade Center favors luxury hulls, expedition craft, civilian logistics ships, and service equipment over front-line ordnance.

name: Yunze Ordnance Charter Office
desc: A Yunze charter office is part arms bureau, part police academy, and part procurement court. Its clerks speak in delivery schedules and threat assessments, tying local security forces to Yunze's disciplined ordnance network. Colonies under such a charter tend to become orderly, heavily documented, and difficult to intimidate. Unlike the other charters, the Yunting Ordnance Exchange is primarily a weapons dealer, stocking deep Yunze missile, energy, kinetic, and point-defense inventories with only a small contract-hull shelf.

name: Monolith Ordnance Charter Office
desc: Monolith Ordnance treats a charter as permission to reinforce the colony before anyone admits it is vulnerable. Survey teams map kill-zones, contractors bury hardened conduits, and quiet workshops turn civilian foundations into military-grade redoubts. The result is not graceful, but it is very hard to break. Its licensed market leans toward armored Monolith hulls, military refits, defensive hardware, and contract warships rather than civilian abundance.

name: Flicker Aviation Charter Office
desc: Flicker Aviation builds its office around hangar clocks, flight-test waivers, and dispatch boards that never sleep. Its charter brings carrier captains, aerospace crews, and logistics brokers into the same rooms as colonial planners, turning a quiet port into a place where ships arrive early and leave with purpose. Its Flight Bazaar specializes in carrier hulls, LPCs, fighter support gear, and aerospace hardware for fleets built around launch decks.
